title: test
author: CodingX-lab

title: Ruby installation fails due to missing libyaml-dev dependency
author: dev-gios
body:
## Problem Description
When installing Ruby via rbenv (version 3.3.5), the installation process fails with a "Failed to build gem native extension" error related to the Psych gem. This occurs because the libyaml-dev library is not included in the initial dependency installation instructions.

## Current Behavior
- Ruby installation fails with Psych gem errors
- Users need to manually troubleshoot and install libyaml-dev
- Installation process requires additional steps not documented in the guide

## Expected Behavior
- Ruby should install without errors on the first attempt
- All necessary dependencies should be included in the initial setup instructions
- The installation process should be smooth for both Ubuntu and Windows/WSL users

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Follow current installation guide
2. Run `rbenv install 3.3.5`
3. Observe the installation failure related to Psych

## Proposed Solution
Add libyaml-dev to the initial dependency installation command:
```bash
sudo apt install -y build-essential tklib zlib1g-dev libssl-dev libffi-dev libxml2 libxml2-dev libxslt1-dev libreadline-dev libyaml-dev

title: Extension VScode is not maintained anymore: SQLite (might be relevant for Data too)
author: v-natalia
body:
It seems like the extension [SQLite](https://github.com/AlexCovizzi/vscode-sqlite/blob/master/README.md) 
is not maintained since mid 2022. In theory it should work, but there was a student having issues to use the extension.

`Failed to open database '/home/thomas/code/ThomasWEBDEV/fullstack-challenges/03-AR-Database/01-DB-and-SQL/03-Interacting-with-code/lib/db/jukebox.sqlite': Parse error near line 4: no such column: "table" - should this be a string literal in single-quotes? aster WHERE (type="table" OR type="view") error here ---^`

I haven't investigated this issue any longer. 
Maybe we should explore changing this extension?

cc @oyohimura

title: Ensure Consistent PostgreSQL Startup Configuration Across Linux and WSL
author: gabrielbdornas
body:
The Le wagon Windows setup has an `postgresql` startup configuration process on `~/.zshrc` file:

https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/e43875127f62e368347db1d538a64f5bd79549d5/windows.md?plain=1#L1178-L1180

And the same not happened on [Linux Ubuntu setup](https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/ubuntu.md).

This could be a problem for those, like me, who aim to use the same setup in more than one operating system[^1].

As pointed by [chatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/share/67dece2e-2de8-8003-a6a4-bb5e32880aa5):

> In a traditional Linux installation, PostgreSQL is often set up as a system service using systemd, meaning it starts automatically when the system boots. 
> **However, WSL does not use systemd by default, so services like PostgreSQL do not start automatically when you open a WSL terminal**.
> WSL has a different init system, which means you need to start services like PostgreSQL manually using `sudo /etc/init.d/postgresql start`.

Thinking about that, I would like to suggest an improvement to equalize both instructions with the same approach. This approach could be, for example, an if statement to check what kind of system we are at the moment.

Since the pure Linux machine and the Windows with WSL (Linux inside Windows) report themselves as Linux, checking the operating system alone won't be enough. Instead, as inside WSL the file `/proc/sys/kernel/osrelease` contains "Microsoft", we can detect whether you're inside WSL by checking for the WSL environment[^2].

So, both instructions could be designed to create the scripts below on the `.zshrc` file, instead of just `sudo /etc/init.d/postgresql start` on WSL setup. This will ensure running PostgreSQL only if I'm inside WSL.

```
if grep -qi microsoft /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease; then
    # We are inside WSL
    sudo /etc/init.d/postgresql start
fi
```

To achieve that, we could update the WSL instructions and add to Ubuntu Linux one something like:

```
echo -e '\n# Inside WSL, start PostgreSQL manually\nif grep -qi microsoft /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease; then\n    sudo /etc/init.d/postgresql start\nfi' >> ~/.zshrc
```

I will be more than happy to open a PR if it makes sense to you guys.

[^1]: I've been using the Linux operating system for personal use since Le Wagon bootcamp, but unfortunately Windows at work with WSL.
[^2]: Also [chatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/share/67dece2e-2de8-8003-a6a4-bb5e32880aa5) help.

title: Problem Installing and Configuring Oh My Zsh on Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS
author: Andresutro
body:
Hello everyone,

I am following the installation procedure provided to set up an environment on Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS on a partition of my PC. However, I encountered an issue when trying to install and configure Oh My Zsh as the default shell.

According to the setup instructions, Oh My Zsh should be configured as the default shell automatically. However, this did not happen in my case. Even after running the command `chsh -s /bin/zsh`, the shell did not switch to Oh My Zsh, and Bash remained the default shell. I investigated further and found a potential manual workaround, which I will describe below.

## Steps to Reproduce the Issue:

1. Install Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS.
2. Follow the setup instructions to install Oh My Zsh.
3. Run the command `chsh -s /bin/zsh`.
4. Restart the terminal and observe that Bash is still the default shell.

## Temporary Solution I Found:

1. In the terminal (using Bash), go to **Terminal Preferences**.
2. Select the **default profile**.
3. Enable the option: **"Run a custom command instead of my shell"**.
4. In the **"Custom command"** field, enter: `zsh`.
5. In the terminal, run the command: `chsh -s $(which zsh)`.
6. Close and reopen the terminal.

This resolved the issue for me, and Oh My Zsh started working correctly.


- Is this behavior expected on Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS?  
- Should an additional step be included in the setup documentation to prevent this issue?  
- If this problem was caused by a mistake on my part, I would appreciate guidance on how to properly configure the shell.

I hope this information is helpful and contributes to improving the installation experience.

Thank you for your attention.

title: Check the locale position
author: v-natalia
body:
Hey!

[Check the locale](https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/windows.md#check-the-locale)
This step in setup is way to ahead, the students  are getting errors because apt is not updated yet, I suggest moving it to 
after [ZSH installation](https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/windows.md#oh-my-zsh)

Also, the section:

> Generate locale
> Please, run this lines in your terminal.
> 
> sudo update-locale LANG=en_US.UTF8
> sudo apt-get install language-pack-en language-pack-en-base manpages
> exec zsh
> You can now close this terminal window.

Should be added to the data-setup, and moved to the same part :pray:

title: WINDOWS: fix warning "bash: warning: setlocale: LC_ALL: cannot change locale (en_US.utf-8)
author: v-natalia
body:
Hi, 
Many students get this warning after running the commands of the section locale:

> 
bash: warning: setlocale: LC_ALL: cannot change locale (en_US.utf-8)

I've seen this in students with laptops configured to use other languages different than english, and so they get this warning even after generating the locale. 
I propose to add the following paragraph to the section [locale](https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/windows.md#check-the-locale)

```

If after, you receive a warning ("bash: warning: setlocale: LC_ALL: cannot change locale (en_US.utf-8)) in your terminal, please do the following:

<details>
  <summary>Generate locale</summary>
Please, run this lines in your terminal.
```bash
sudo update-locale LANG=en_US.UTF8
sudo apt-get install language-pack-en language-pack-en-base manpages
exec zsh
```

</details>
```

title: Different postgresql message
author: v-natalia
body:
Setup postgresql
https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/windows.md#postgresql
The guide says:

✔️ If you see a * Starting PostgreSQL 14 database server message, you're good to go 👍 
we should add that the message  **"Starting postgresql (via systemctl): postgresql.service."**  is also fine!

title: Set default nodeJS 18.18.0
author: carlosferrerdev
body:
On September 11, 2023, NodeJS 16 was discontinued and will no longer receive support or security updates. 

Update the kitt material to install the latest stable LTS version of NodeJS, which is version 18.18.0

title: Setting `BROWSER` variable section is confusing right now
author: SebiBasti
body:
In the current implementation of the setup the section `Linking your default browser to Ubuntu` causes a lot of tickets. In yesterdays `windows setup day` for Germany we had many problems with this because students collapse the section for their specific browser and then execute all the commands and unfortunately often don't realise the `If you get an error like` condition. We've been discussing different ways of formating this:

- we could hide the `if you get an error like` section in another collapsable
- maybe we add some more highlighting, additional line breaks or some red color to get the attention of the student
- we could completely rewrite the section to prevent confusion

I wanted to open this issue to start a discussion on how we could make this section better. @v-natalia, @mdik & @ElvisDot  anything you want to add?

title: Locale section should specify Ubuntu terminal
author: v-natalia
body:
In this step https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/windows.md#check-the-locale

> The locale is a mechanism allowing to customize programs to your language and country.
> 
> Let's verify that the default locale is set to english:
> 


Should be:

> The locale is a mechanism allowing to customize programs to your language and country.
> 
> Let's verify that the default locale is set to english, please type this in **the Ubuntu terminal**:
> 

Many thanks :pray:

title: Slack test is not working anymore
author: v-natalia
body:
In this step, the section 'Settings' https://github.com/lewagon/setup/blob/master/windows.md#slack 
This shortcut is not working `In any channel message bar type /call --test and press Enter`
The guide from Slack suggests these steps https://slack.com/help/articles/115003538426-Troubleshoot-audio-and-video-issues-in-Slack#run-our-test
1. From your desktop, click your profile picture in the top right.
2. Select Preferences from the menu.
2. Click Audio & video in the left-side column.
4. Below Troubleshooting, click Run an audio, video and screensharing test. The test will open in a new window.
5. Check that your preferred speaker, microphone and camera devices appear in the drop-down menus, then click Start test

I suggest that we change it to that :rocket:

title: Audit node / webpack / yarn from the setup
author: trandthanh
body:
Part of the **More JS in Web Dev Syllabus** (moving towards a full focus on the front-end part of JS and `importmap`), let's remove any mention to the `webpack` bundler and `yarn`. Keeping `node.js` might come in handy, so keeping it might be a safe move.

Tasks:
- [x] Audit mentions of `webpack` to remove them
- [x] Audit mentions of `yarn` to remove them
- [x] Audit mentions of `node.js` to see what is worth keeping

title: pyenv homebrew and pip path
author: TulipeSoyeuse
body: found out yesterday that for some students, ( all of them mac m1 ) homebrew pip is referenced in the path before pyenv. they had a python version installed ( by default i guess ) with homebrew. we fixed it by putting the pyenv path export at the end of zshrc insted of zprofile
